pull_request.title: feat: add Node.js bindings via SWIG NAPI backend
pull_request.body:
## Summary

- Add Node.js/NAPI bindings using SWIG's `-javascript -napi` backend
- The existing `xtract.i` interface works as-is — only change is a C++-compatible cast in `create_filterbank`
- Add `node` target and `check-node` to `swig/Makefile`
- Add `test.js` mirroring `test.py` and `test.java` (mean, variance, spectrum, stateful functions)
- Add `node-addon-api` install step to CI for Linux and macOS

### Usage

```bash
cd swig
npm install node-addon-api
make node
node test.js
```

### Dependencies

- Node.js 14+ with headers
- `node-addon-api` (installed via npm)
- SWIG 4.1+ (NAPI support)

Closes #26

## Test plan

- [x] `make && make check` passes (375 assertions, 61 test cases)
- [x] `make -C swig check` — Python, Java, and Node.js tests all pass on macOS
- [ ] Verify on Linux CI
- [ ] Verify Node.js test on CI

changed_files.patch:
@@ -23,12 +23,18 @@ jobs:
           distribution: temurin
           java-version: 17
 
+      - name: Setup Node.js (Linux/macOS)
+        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: 22
+
       - name: Setup MSYS2 (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         uses: msys2/setup-msys2@v2
         with:
           msystem: MINGW64
-          install: mingw-w64-x86_64-gcc mingw-w64-x86_64-python mingw-w64-x86_64-swig make
+          install: mingw-w64-x86_64-gcc mingw-w64-x86_64-python mingw-w64-x86_64-swig mingw-w64-x86_64-nodejs make
 
       - name: Install SWIG (Linux)
         if: runner.os == 'Linux'
@@ -57,20 +63,34 @@ jobs:
           make
           make check
 
+      - name: Install Node.js headers (Linux/macOS)
+        if: runner.os != 'Windows'
+        run: |
+          NODE_VER=$(node -v | tr -d v)
+          curl -sL "https://nodejs.org/dist/v${NODE_VER}/node-v${NODE_VER}-headers.tar.gz" | tar xz
+          echo "NODE_INCLUDE=$(pwd)/node-v${NODE_VER}/include/node" >> $GITHUB_ENV
+          npm install --prefix swig node-addon-api
+
       - name: SWIG bindings (Linux)
         if: runner.os == 'Linux'
         run: make -C swig check
         env:
           JAVA_HOME: ${{ env.JAVA_HOME }}
+          NODE_INCLUDE: ${{ env.NODE_INCLUDE }}
           PYTHON: python3
 
       - name: SWIG bindings (macOS)
         if: runner.os == 'macOS'
         run: make -C swig check
         env:
           JAVA_HOME: ${{ env.JAVA_HOME }}
+          NODE_INCLUDE: ${{ env.NODE_INCLUDE }}
 
       - name: SWIG bindings (Windows)
         if: runner.os == 'Windows'
         shell: msys2 {0}
-        run: PYTHON=python make -C swig check-python
+        run: |
+          npm install --prefix swig node-addon-api
+          export JAVA_HOME="$(cygpath -u "$JAVA_HOME")"
+          export PATH="${JAVA_HOME}/bin:${PATH}"
+          PYTHON=python NODE_INCLUDE=/mingw64/include/node make -C swig check

@@ -12,19 +12,34 @@ ifeq ($(OS), Darwin)
     CFLAGS=-g -c
     LDFLAGS=-bundle -flat_namespace -undefined suppress
     LIBS=-framework Accelerate
+    NODE_LIBS=
     JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX=dylib
+    JAVA_LIB_PREFIX=libj
     LIBXTRACT=../src/lib$(NAME).a
     LINK_LIB=$(LIBXTRACT)
+else ifneq (,$(findstring MINGW,$(OS)))
+    CFLAGS=-g -c
+    LDFLAGS=-shared
+    LIBS=-lm
+    NODE_LIBS=-lnode
+    JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX=dll
+    JAVA_LIB_PREFIX=j
+    LIBXTRACT=$(wildcard ../src/lib$(NAME).a ../src/lib$(NAME).lib)
+    LINK_LIB=-Wl,--whole-archive $(LIBXTRACT) -Wl,--no-whole-archive
 else
     CFLAGS=-g -c -fPIC
     LDFLAGS=-shared
     LIBS=-lm
+    NODE_LIBS=
     JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX=so
+    JAVA_LIB_PREFIX=libj
     LIBXTRACT=$(wildcard ../src/lib$(NAME).a ../src/lib$(NAME).lib)
     LINK_LIB=-Wl,--whole-archive $(LIBXTRACT) -Wl,--no-whole-archive
 endif
 
-.PHONY: python java check check-python check-java
+NAPI_INCLUDE = $(shell node -e "console.log(require('node-addon-api').include)" 2>/dev/null | tr -d '"')
+
+.PHONY: python java node check check-python check-java check-node
 
 python:
 	@swig -I../include -python $(NAME).i
@@ -38,10 +53,18 @@ endif
 	@mkdir -p java
 	@swig -I../include -java -package $(NAME) -outdir java $(NAME).i
 	@$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(NAME)_wrap.c -o $(NAME)_wrap.o -I$(JAVA_HOME)/include -I$(wildcard $(JAVA_HOME)/include/darwin $(JAVA_HOME)/include/linux $(JAVA_HOME)/include/win32) -I../include
-	@$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) $(LINK_LIB) $(NAME)_wrap.o -o libj$(NAME).$(JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX) $(LIBS)
+	@$(CC) $(LDFLAGS) $(LINK_LIB) $(NAME)_wrap.o -o $(JAVA_LIB_PREFIX)$(NAME).$(JAVA_LIB_SUFFIX) $(LIBS)
 	@javac java/*.java
 
-check: check-python check-java
+node:
+ifndef NODE_INCLUDE
+	$(error NODE_INCLUDE is not set. Set it to your Node.js include directory containing node_api.h)
+endif
+	@swig -I../include -javascript -napi $(NAME).i
+	@$(CXX) -std=c++17 $(CFLAGS) $(NAME)_wrap.c -o $(NAME)_wrap.o -I$(NODE_INCLUDE) -I$(NAPI_INCLUDE) -I../include
+	@$(CXX) $(LDFLAGS) $(LINK_LIB) $(NAME)_wrap.o -o $(NAME).node $(LIBS) $(NODE_LIBS)
+
+check: check-python check-java check-node
 
 check-python: python
 	@echo "Running Python bindings test..."
@@ -55,6 +78,15 @@ check-java: java
 	@java -Djava.library.path=. -cp . test
 	@$(RM) -r $(NAME) test.class
 
+check-node:
+ifdef NODE_INCLUDE
+	@$(MAKE) node
+	@echo "Running Node.js bindings test..."
+	@node test.js
+else
+	@echo "Skipping Node.js bindings test (NODE_INCLUDE not set)"
+endif
+
 install: python
 	@SITE_DIR=$$($(PYTHON) -c "import site; print(site.getsitepackages()[0])") && \
 	install -d "$$SITE_DIR" && \
@@ -66,6 +98,8 @@ clean:
 	@$(RM) *.pyc
 	@$(RM) *.so
 	@$(RM) *.dylib
+	@$(RM) *.dll
+	@$(RM) *.node
 	@$(RM) $(NAME)_wrap.c
 	@$(RM) $(NAME).py
 	@$(RM) -r java

commit_summaries.message:
feat: add Node.js bindings via SWIG NAPI backend

Add Node.js/NAPI bindings using SWIG's -javascript -napi backend.
The existing xtract.i interface works as-is — no SWIG interface
changes needed beyond a C++ compatible cast in create_filterbank.

- Add node target to swig/Makefile
- Add test.js mirroring test.py and test.java (mean, variance,
  spectrum, stateful functions)
- Add node-addon-api install step and check-node to CI
- Requires node-addon-api: npm install node-addon-api in swig/

Closes #26
fix: gitignore all generated SWIG build artifacts

fix: install Node.js headers on CI for NAPI compilation

GitHub runners have Node.js runtime but not development headers.
Use node-gyp install to fetch headers, and update NODE_INCLUDE
in Makefile to check both the prefix path and ~/.node-gyp fallback.

fix: simplify Node.js include path handling

Require NODE_INCLUDE to be set explicitly, matching the JAVA_HOME
pattern. CI sets it via node-gyp install path. Remove inline
JavaScript from Makefile.
fix: use ${HOME} not $(HOME) for shell variable expansion in CI

fix: find node-gyp headers reliably, skip check-node gracefully

node-gyp stores headers in platform-dependent cache directories
(~/Library/Caches/node-gyp on macOS, ~/.cache/node-gyp on Linux).
Use find to locate the actual path after node-gyp install.

Make check-node skip gracefully when NODE_INCLUDE is not set
(e.g. on Windows) instead of erroring.

fix: run all SWIG binding tests on all platforms

- Linux/macOS: download Node.js headers directly from nodejs.org
  instead of using node-gyp (simpler, no platform-dependent cache)
- Windows: add mingw-w64-x86_64-nodejs to MSYS2 for Node.js headers
  at /mingw64/include/node, run full check (Python + Java + Node)
- All three platforms now run make -C swig check
fix: convert Windows JAVA_HOME to MSYS2 path with cygpath
fix: add Java bin directory to PATH in MSYS2 shell

fix: Windows Java native library naming (jxtract.dll not libjxtract.so)

System.loadLibrary("jxtract") looks for jxtract.dll on Windows
(no lib prefix), libjxtract.dylib on macOS, libjxtract.so on Linux.
Add MINGW detection to produce correct output name per platform.

fix: link Node.js extension against libnode on Windows

On Windows, NAPI symbols must be resolved at link time.
Add -lnode for MINGW builds (provided by mingw-w64-x86_64-nodejs).
